name: pica_gmail_list_and_view_read_or_unread_emails
description: [GMAIL] List and View Read or Unread Emails on gmail

numberOfEmails.description: Number of emails to retrieve (1-100, default: 10)

pageToken.description: Token to retrieve the next page of results (from previous response's `nextPageToken`)

_pica_metadata.action_title: List and View Read or Unread Emails

name: pica_gmail_list_and_view_email_threads
description: [GMAIL] List and View Email Threads on gmail

numberOfThreads.description: Number of threads to retrieve (1-50, default: 10)

pageToken.description: Page token for pagination to retrieve the next set of threads

query.description: Gmail search query to filter threads (e.g., "from:example@gmail.com")

name: pica_gmail_list_and_view_email_drafts
description: [GMAIL] List and View Email Drafts on gmail

numberOfDrafts.description: Number of drafts to retrieve (1-50, default: 10)

pageToken.description: Page token for pagination to retrieve the next set of drafts

query.description: Gmail search query to filter drafts (e.g., "from:me@example.com")

name: pica_gmail_send_email
description: [GMAIL] Send Email on gmail

bcc.description: BCC recipients. Can be a comma-separated string or array

body.description: Email body content (plain text or HTML). Plain text is auto-converted to HTML

cc.description: CC recipients. Can be a comma-separated string or array

inReplyTo.description: Message-ID of the email being replied to. Used for proper email threading in email clients

isHtml.description: Whether the body is already HTML (default: false). When false, plain text is auto-converted to HTML with \n mapped to

references.description: Space-separated list of Message-IDs for the email chain. Used for proper email threading

threadId.description: ID of the thread to reply to. Use this to continue an existing email conversation

to.description: Email recipient(s). Can be a comma-separated string or array

userId.description: The user's email address or "me" for authenticated user (default: "me")

name: pica_gmail_create_draft
description: [GMAIL] Create Draft on gmail

body.description: Email body content (plain text or HTML). Plain text is auto-converted to HTML

inReplyTo.description: Message-ID of the email being replied to. Used for proper email threading in email clients

isHtml.description: Whether the body is already HTML (default: false). When false, plain text is auto-converted to HTML with \n mapped to

labelIds.description: Gmail label IDs to apply to the draft

references.description: Space-separated list of Message-IDs for the email chain. Used for proper email threading

threadId.description: ID of the thread to add the draft to. Use this to create a reply draft

userId.description: Gmail user ID (default: "me" for authenticated user)

name: pica_gmail_batch_delete_a_user_s_gmail_messages
description: [GMAIL] Batch Delete a User’s Gmail Messages on gmail

ids.description: The IDs of the messages to delete. Array items are strings (message IDs).

_pica_metadata.action_title: Batch Delete a User’s Gmail Messages

name: pica_gmail_create_a_user_s_draft
description: [GMAIL] Create a User’s Draft on gmail

classificationLabelValues.description: Classification Label values on the message (Google Workspace accounts only). Each classification label ID must be unique; duplicates are reduced arbitrarily.

fields.description: Field values for the given classification label ID.

fieldId.description: Required (within this object). Field ID for the Classification Label Value.

selection.description: Selection choice ID (only if the field type is `SELECTION`).

labelId.description: Required (within this object). Canonical or raw alphanumeric classification label ID.

historyId.description: ID of the last history record that modified this message.

id.description: The immutable ID of the message.

internalDate.description: Internal message creation timestamp (epoch ms) used for inbox ordering.

labelIds.description: List of IDs of labels applied to this message.

body.description: The body of a single MIME message part.

attachmentId.description: If present, contains the ID of an external attachment retrievable via `messages.attachments.get`. If absent, the full content is in `data`.

data.description: Message-part body data as a base64url encoded string. May be empty for container MIME types or when sent as a separate attachment.

size.description: Number of bytes for the message part data (encoding notwithstanding).

body.title: Message Part Body

filename.description: Filename of the attachment. Only present if this part represents an attachment.

headers.description: List of headers on this message part. For the top-level message part, it contains standard RFC 2822 headers such as `To`, `From`, and `Subject`.

mimeType.description: MIME type of the message part.

partId.description: The immutable ID of the message part.

parts.description: Child MIME parts. Applies to container MIME parts (e.g., `multipart/*`). For non-container types (e.g., `text/plain`), this is empty.

raw.description: The entire email message in RFC 2822 format, base64url encoded.

sizeEstimate.description: Estimated size in bytes of the message.

snippet.description: A short part of the message text.

threadId.description: The ID of the thread the message belongs to. To add a message/draft to a thread: (1) specify `threadId` on the `Message`/`Draft.message`, (2) set `References` and `In-Reply-To` headers per RFC 2822, (3) `Subject` headers must match.

name: pica_gmail_delete_a_user_s_draft
description: [GMAIL] Delete a User's Draft on gmail

path.description: Path parameters to call DELETE /gmail/v1/users/{userId}/drafts/{id}

id.description: The ID of the draft to delete.

userId.description: The user's email address. The special value `me` can be used to indicate the authenticated user. Default: "me".

path.title: Path Parameters

name: pica_gmail_list_a_user_s_drafts
description: [GMAIL] List a User’s Drafts on gmail

path.description: Path parameters to send in the URL path.

userId.description: The user's email address. The special value `me` can be used to indicate the authenticated user. Default: `me`.

path.title: Path Parameters

includeSpamTrash.description: Include drafts from `SPAM` and `TRASH` in the results. Default: `false`.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of drafts to return. Default: `100`. Maximum allowed value: `500`. (uint32)

pageToken.description: Page token to retrieve a specific page of results in the list.

q.description: Only return draft messages matching the specified query. Supports the same query format as the Gmail search box (e.g. "from:someuser@example.com rfc822msgid: is:unread").

name: pica_gmail_list_a_user_s_gmail_history
description: [GMAIL] List a User's Gmail History on gmail

path.description: Parameters substituted into the request URL path.

userId.description: The user's email address. The special value `me` can be used to indicate the authenticated user. Default: `me`.

path.title: Path Parameters

query.description: Parameters sent on the URL query string.

historyTypes.description: History types to be returned. (Repeated query parameter.)

labelId.description: Only return messages with a label matching this ID.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of history records to return. Default: 100. Max allowed: 500. Format: uint32.

pageToken.description: Page token to retrieve a specific page of results.

startHistoryId.description: Required. Returns history records after the specified startHistoryId. The supplied startHistoryId should be obtained from the historyId of a message, thread, or previous list response. Format: uint64.

query.title: Query Parameters

name: pica_gmail_create_a_user_s_gmail_label
description: [GMAIL] Create a User’s Gmail Label on gmail

color.description: The color to assign to the label. Color is only available for labels that have their `type` set to `user`.

backgroundColor.description: Background color as hex `#RRGGBB`. Required to set label color. Only a predefined set of values are allowed (per docs).

textColor.description: Text color as hex `#RRGGBB`. Required to set label color. Only a predefined set of values are allowed (per docs).

color.title: Label Color

id.description: Immutable ID of the label. (Not required for create in the provided docs.)

labelListVisibility.description: Visibility of the label in the Gmail web UI label list.

messageListVisibility.description: Visibility of messages with this label in the Gmail web UI message list.

messagesTotal.description: Total number of messages with the label.

messagesUnread.description: Number of unread messages with the label.

name.description: The display name of the label.

threadsTotal.description: Total number of threads with the label.

threadsUnread.description: Number of unread threads with the label.

type.description: Owner type for the label. Allowed values: `system`, `user`. (System labels cannot be added/modified/deleted; behavior may be restricted.)

name: pica_gmail_get_a_user_s_gmail_label
description: [GMAIL] Get a User's Gmail Label on gmail

path.description: Parameters sent in the URL path.

id.description: The ID of the label to retrieve. Sent in the URL path.

userId.description: The user's email address. The special value `me` can be used to indicate the authenticated user. Default: `me`. Sent in the URL path.

path.title: Path Parameters

name: pica_gmail_list_a_user_s_gmail_labels
description: [GMAIL] List a User’s Gmail Labels on gmail

path.description: Parameters to be interpolated into /gmail/v1/users/{userId}/labels. If the user does not provide a userId, default it explicitly to "me".

userId.description: The user's email address. The special value `me` can be used to indicate the authenticated user. Default value: "me".

path.title: Path Parameters

name: pica_gmail_patch_a_user_s_gmail_label
description: [GMAIL] Patch a User’s Gmail Label on gmail

color.description: The color to assign to the label. Color is only available for labels that have their type set to user.

backgroundColor.description: Background color as hex #RRGGBB. Must be one of Gmail’s predefined color values (as documented). Required to set label color.

textColor.description: Text color as hex. Must be one of Gmail’s predefined color values (as documented). Required to set label color.

id.description: The immutable ID of the label. (Included in schema; typically not something you change.)

labelListVisibility.description: Visibility of the label in the Gmail web UI label list.

messageListVisibility.description: Visibility of messages with this label in the Gmail web UI message list.

messagesTotal.description: Total number of messages with the label. (Read-only in practice; included in schema.)

messagesUnread.description: Number of unread messages with the label. (Read-only in practice; included in schema.)

name.description: The display name of the label.

threadsTotal.description: Total number of threads with the label. (Read-only in practice; included in schema.)

threadsUnread.description: Number of unread threads with the label. (Read-only in practice; included in schema.)

type.description: Owner type for the label. Allowed values: system, user. (System labels cannot be modified.)

name: pica_gmail_update_a_user_s_label
description: [GMAIL] Update a User's Label on gmail

color.description: The color to assign to the label. Only available for labels with type = user.

backgroundColor.description: Background hex color #RRGGBB (e.g., #000000). Required to set color. Must be one of Gmail’s predefined color values (see docs list).

textColor.description: Text hex color. Required to set color. Must be one of Gmail’s predefined color values (see docs list).

id.description: The immutable ID of the label. Required for this update method. (Send in request body.)

labelListVisibility.description: Visibility of the label in the Gmail web UI label list. Required for gmail.users.labels.update.

messageListVisibility.description: Visibility of messages with this label in the Gmail web UI message list. Required for gmail.users.labels.update.

messagesTotal.description: Total number of messages with the label.

messagesUnread.description: Number of unread messages with the label.

name.description: The display name of the label. Required for gmail.users.labels.update.

threadsTotal.description: Total number of threads with the label.

threadsUnread.description: Number of unread threads with the label.

type.description: Owner type for the label. Allowed values: system, user. (Note: system labels cannot be modified/deleted; see Notes.)

name: pica_gmail_get_a_user_s_gmail_message
description: [GMAIL] Get a User's Gmail Message on gmail

Accept.description: Media type accepted by the client.

path.description: Path parameters for GET /gmail/v1/users/{userId}/messages/{id}

id.description: The ID of the message to retrieve. This ID is usually retrieved using `messages.list`. The ID is also contained in results when a message is inserted (`messages.insert`) or imported (`messages.import`).

userId.description: The user's email address. The special value `me` can be used to indicate the authenticated user. Default: `me`.

path.title: Path Parameters

query.description: Optional query parameters for shaping the response.

format.description: The format to return the message in. Default: `full`. Possible values: `minimal`, `full`, `raw`, `metadata`.

metadataHeaders.description: When given and `format` is `METADATA`, only include headers specified. (Repeat the query parameter to specify multiple headers.)

query.title: Query Parameters

name: pica_gmail_import_a_user_s_message
description: [GMAIL] Import a User’s Message on gmail

classificationLabelValues.description: Classification Label values on the message. Only used for Google Workspace accounts. Each classification label ID must be unique; duplicates may be arbitrarily reduced to one.

fields.description: Field values for the given classification label ID.

fieldId.description: Required (within this object). Field ID for the Classification Label Value (maps to Google Drive `Label.Field` ID).

selection.description: Selection choice ID (only if the field type is `SELECTION` in Google Drive `Label.Field`).

labelId.description: Required (within this object). Canonical or raw alphanumeric classification label ID (maps to Google Drive Labels API label ID).

historyId.description: The ID of the last history record that modified this message.

id.description: The immutable ID of the message.

internalDate.description: The internal message creation timestamp (epoch ms), which determines ordering in the inbox. For API-migrated mail, it can be configured to be based on the `Date` header.

labelIds.description: List of IDs of labels applied to this message. Items are strings.

body.description: The body of a single MIME message part.

attachmentId.description: If present, ID of an external attachment retrievable via `messages.attachments.get`. If absent, the entire content is in `payload.body.data`.

data.description: Body data as a base64url encoded string. May be empty for container MIME types or when data is sent as a separate attachment (then `attachmentId` is present).

size.description: Number of bytes for the message part data (encoding notwithstanding).

body.title: MessagePartBody

filename.description: The filename of the attachment. Only present if this message part represents an attachment.

headers.description: List of headers on this message part. For the top-level message part, this contains standard RFC 2822 headers such as `To`, `From`, and `Subject`.

mimeType.description: The MIME type of the message part.

partId.description: The immutable ID of the message part.

parts.description: Child MIME message parts (applies to container parts like `multipart/*`). For non-container types like `text/plain`, this is empty.

raw.description: The entire email message in an RFC 2822 formatted and base64url encoded string.

sizeEstimate.description: Estimated size in bytes of the message.

snippet.description: A short part of the message text.

threadId.description: The ID of the thread the message belongs to. To add a message or draft to a thread: (1) `threadId` must be specified, (2) `References` and `In-Reply-To` headers must comply with RFC 2822, and (3) `Subject` headers must match.

name: pica_gmail_insert_a_message_into_a_user_s_mailbox
description: [GMAIL] Insert a Message Into a User’s Mailbox on gmail

classificationLabelValues.description: Classification Label values on the message (Google Workspace only). Each classification label ID must be unique; duplicates are arbitrarily reduced to one.

fields.description: Field values for the given classification label ID.

fieldId.description: Required (per schema description). Field ID for the classification label value (maps to Google Drive `Label.Field` ID).

selection.description: Selection choice ID (only set if the field type is `SELECTION` in the Google Drive label schema).

labelId.description: Required (per schema description). Canonical or raw alphanumeric classification label ID (maps to Google Drive Label resource ID).

historyId.description: The ID of the last history record that modified this message.

id.description: The immutable ID of the message.

internalDate.description: Internal message creation timestamp (epoch ms) used for ordering.

labelIds.description: List of IDs of labels applied to this message.

body.description: The body of a single MIME message part.

attachmentId.description: If present, ID of an external attachment retrievable via `messages.attachments.get`. If absent, entire content is in `payload.body.data`.

data.description: Body data as a base64url encoded string. May be empty for container MIME types or when stored as a separate attachment (then `attachmentId` is present).

size.description: Number of bytes for the message part data (encoding notwithstanding).

body.title: Message Part Body

filename.description: Filename of the attachment (only present if this part represents an attachment).

headers.description: List of headers on this message part (e.g., `To`, `From`, `Subject`). Each item contains `name` and `value`.

mimeType.description: MIME type of the message part.

partId.description: The immutable ID of the message part.

parts.description: Child MIME message parts (applies to container MIME types like `multipart/*`).

raw.description: The entire email message in RFC 2822 format, base64url encoded. (Annotated as required for this method in the schema annotations list.)

sizeEstimate.description: Estimated size in bytes of the message.

snippet.description: A short part of the message text.

threadId.description: The ID of the thread the message belongs to. To add a message/draft to a thread: (1) specify `threadId` in the request, (2) set `References` and `In-Reply-To` headers in compliance with RFC 2822, and (3) `Subject` headers must match.

_pica_metadata.action_title: Insert a Message Into a User’s Mailbox

name: pica_gmail_list_a_user_s_gmail_messages
description: [GMAIL] List a User’s Gmail Messages on gmail

headers.description: HTTP headers to include with the request.
headers.title: Headers

path.description: Parameters to be substituted into the request URL path.

userId.description: The user's email address. The special value `me` can be used to indicate the authenticated user. Default: `me`.

path.title: Path Parameters

query.description: Optional query parameters for filtering and pagination.

includeSpamTrash.description: Include messages from `SPAM` and `TRASH` in the results. Default: `false`.

labelIds.description: Only return messages with labels that match all of the specified label IDs. This parameter is repeatable.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of messages to return. Default: `100`. Maximum allowed value: `500`.

pageToken.description: Page token to retrieve a specific page of results in the list.

q.description: Only return messages matching the specified query. Supports the same query format as the Gmail search box (e.g., "from:someuser@example.com rfc822msgid: is:unread"). Cannot be used when accessing the API using the `gmail.metadata` scope.

query.title: Query Parameters

name: pica_gmail_modify_a_user_s_message_labels
description: [GMAIL] Modify a User's Message Labels on gmail

addLabelIds.description: A list of IDs of labels to add to this message. You can add up to 100 labels with each update.

removeLabelIds.description: A list of IDs of labels to remove from this message. You can remove up to 100 labels with each update.

name: pica_gmail_trash_a_user_s_gmail_message

name: pica_gmail_send_a_user_s_gmail_message
description: [GMAIL] Send a User's Gmail Message on gmail

classificationLabelValues.description: Classification label values (Google Workspace accounts only). Duplicate label IDs: only one retained arbitrarily.

fields.description: Field values for the given classification label ID.

fieldId.description: Required (within this object). Field ID for the classification label value (maps to Drive `Label.Field` ID).

selection.description: Selection choice ID (only if the Drive label field type is `SELECTION`).

labelId.description: Required (within this object). Canonical or raw alphanumeric classification label ID (maps to Drive Labels ID).

historyId.description: ID of the last history record that modified this message.

id.description: Immutable ID of the message.

internalDate.description: Internal creation timestamp (epoch ms) used for ordering.

labelIds.description: List of IDs of Gmail labels applied to this message.

payload.description: A single MIME message part (structured representation of the message).

body.description: Body of a single MIME message part.

attachmentId.description: If present, ID of an external attachment retrievable via `messages.attachments.get`.

data.description: Body data of this MIME part as base64url. May be empty for container parts or when stored as an attachment.

size.description: Number of bytes for the message part data (encoding notwithstanding).

body.title: MIME Part Body

filename.description: Filename of the attachment (only present if this part represents an attachment).

headers.description: List of headers for this MIME part (top-level typically includes RFC 2822 headers like `To`, `From`, `Subject`).

mimeType.description: MIME type of the message part.

partId.description: Immutable ID of the message part.

parts.description: Child MIME message parts for container MIME types (e.g., `multipart/*`). Empty for non-container types.

items.description: Child MIME part object (structure not further specified in this document).

payload.title: MIME Message Part

raw.description: The entire email message in RFC 2822 format, base64url encoded. Annotated as required for `gmail.users.messages.send`.

sizeEstimate.description: Estimated size in bytes of the message.

snippet.description: A short part of the message text.

threadId.description: Thread ID to add the message to. Criteria: (1) specify this `threadId`; (2) set `References` and `In-Reply-To` per RFC 2822; (3) `Subject` headers must match.

name: pica_gmail_create_a_user_s_client_side_encryption_cse_identity
description: [GMAIL] Create a User’s Client-Side Encryption (CSE) Identity on gmail

emailAddress.description: The email address for the sending identity. The email address must be the primary email address of the authenticated user.

primaryKeyPairId.description: If a key pair is associated, the ID of the key pair (`CseKeyPair`).

signAndEncryptKeyPairs.description: Configuration to use different key pairs for signing and encryption.

encryptionKeyPairId.description: The ID of the `CseKeyPair` that encrypts signed outgoing mail.

signingKeyPairId.description: The ID of the `CseKeyPair` that signs outgoing mail.

signAndEncryptKeyPairs.title: Sign And Encrypt Key Pairs

name: pica_gmail_create_a_user_s_send_mail_as_alias
description: [GMAIL] Create a User’s Send Mail As Alias on gmail

displayName.description: Name that appears in the "From:" header. For custom "from" addresses, if empty, Gmail uses the name from the primary address. If the admin disables name-format updates, updates to the primary login’s name silently fail.

isDefault.description: Whether this address is the default "From:" address. Only legal value clients may write is true (there is exactly one default; setting one to true makes the previous default become false).

replyToAddress.description: Optional email placed in a "Reply-To:" header. If empty, Gmail does not generate a "Reply-To:" header.

sendAsEmail.description: The email address that appears in the "From:" header for mail sent using this alias. Read-only for all operations except create.

signature.description: Optional HTML signature included in messages composed with this alias in Gmail web UI; added to new emails only. Gmail sanitizes this HTML before saving.

smtpMsa.description: Configuration for communication with an SMTP service. If specified, Gmail attempts to connect to validate configuration before creating the alias.

host.description: Hostname of the SMTP service. Required if smtpMsa is provided.

password.description: Password used to authenticate with the SMTP service. Write-only (accepted in requests; never returned in responses).

port.description: Port of the SMTP service. Required if smtpMsa is provided.

securityMode.description: Protocol to secure SMTP communication. Required if smtpMsa is provided.

username.description: Username used to authenticate with the SMTP service. Write-only (accepted in requests; never returned in responses).

smtpMsa.title: SMTP MSA Configuration

treatAsAlias.description: Whether Gmail should treat this address as an alias for the user’s primary email address. Applies only to custom "from" aliases.

_pica_metadata.action_title: Create a User’s Send Mail As Alias

description: [GMAIL] List a User’s Send As Aliases on gmail

path.description: Path parameters to be sent in the URL path.

userId.description: User’s email address. The special value "me" can be used to indicate the authenticated user. Default: "me".

path.title: Path Parameters

_pica_metadata.action_title: List a User’s Send As Aliases

name: pica_gmail_patch_a_user_s_send_as_alias_settings
description: [GMAIL] Patch a User’s Send-As Alias Settings on gmail

displayName.description: Name shown in the From: header. For custom from addresses, if empty Gmail uses the primary address name. If an admin has disabled name format updates, updating this for the primary login will silently fail.

isDefault.description: Whether this address is selected as the default From: address. Every Gmail account has exactly one default send-as address; the only legal value clients may write is true. Setting this to true will make the previous default’s isDefault become false.

isPrimary.description: Whether this is the primary login address. Read-only.

replyToAddress.description: Optional email to include in a Reply-To: header. If empty, Gmail will not generate a Reply-To header.

sendAsEmail.description: The email address that appears in the From: header for this alias. Read-only for all operations except create.

signature.description: Optional HTML signature included in messages composed with this alias in Gmail web UI (added to new emails only).

smtpMsa.description: Configuration for communication with an SMTP service.

host.description: SMTP hostname. Required (if smtpMsa is provided).

password.description: SMTP password. Write-only (accepted in requests; never populated in responses).

port.description: SMTP port. Required (if smtpMsa is provided).

securityMode.description: Security protocol for SMTP. Required (if smtpMsa is provided).

username.description: SMTP username. Write-only (accepted in requests; never populated in responses).

smtpMsa.title: SMTP MSA

treatAsAlias.description: Whether Gmail should treat this address as an alias for the user’s primary email. Only applies to custom “from” aliases.

verificationStatus.description: Verification status for custom “from” aliases. Read-only.

_pica_metadata.action_title: Patch a User’s Send-As Alias Settings

name: pica_gmail_insert_s_mime_info_for_a_user_s_send_as_alias

encryptedKeyPassword.description: Encrypted key password, when the PKCS#12 key is encrypted.

isDefault.description: Whether this SmimeInfo is the default one for this user's send-as address.

pkcs12.description: Required to upload a new S/MIME config. PKCS#12 bundle containing a single private/public key pair and certificate chain. Provide as base64-encoded bytes. This field is accepted on create and is never returned.

name: pica_gmail_update_send_as_alias_settings
description: [GMAIL] Update Send-As Alias Settings on gmail

displayName.description: Name that appears in the `From:` header. For custom from addresses, if empty, Gmail uses the primary address’s name. If admin disables updating name format, updates for the primary login may silently fail.

isDefault.description: Whether this address is selected as the default `From:` address. Every Gmail account has exactly one default send-as address, so the only legal value clients may write is `true`. Setting this to `true` will make the previous default’s `isDefault` become `false`.

replyToAddress.description: Optional email address included in a `Reply-To:` header for mail sent using this alias. If empty, Gmail will not generate a `Reply-To:` header.

sendAsEmail.description: Read-only for all operations except create.

signature.description: Optional HTML signature included in messages composed with this alias in the Gmail web UI (new emails only). Gmail sanitizes provided HTML before saving.

smtpMsa.description: Configuration for communication with an SMTP service.

host.description: Hostname of the SMTP service. (Marked “Required” in schema when `smtpMsa` is used.)

password.description: SMTP password used for authentication. Write-only: may be provided in requests; never populated in responses.

port.description: Port of the SMTP service. (Marked “Required” in schema when `smtpMsa` is used.)

securityMode.description: Security protocol for SMTP. (Marked “Required” in schema when `smtpMsa` is used.)

username.description: SMTP username used for authentication. Write-only: may be provided in requests; never populated in responses.

smtpMsa.title: SMTP MSA

treatAsAlias.description: Whether Gmail should treat this address as an alias for the user’s primary email address. Applies only to custom `from` aliases.

verificationStatus.description: Read-only. Applies only to custom `from` aliases.

name: pica_gmail_update_a_user_s_imap_settings
description: [GMAIL] Update a User’s IMAP Settings on gmail

autoExpunge.description: If true, Gmail immediately expunges a message when it is marked as deleted in IMAP. Otherwise, Gmail waits for an update from the client before expunging messages marked as deleted.

enabled.description: Whether IMAP is enabled for the account.

expungeBehavior.description: The action executed on a message when it is marked as deleted and expunged from the last visible IMAP folder.

maxFolderSize.description: Optional limit on the number of messages an IMAP folder may contain. A value of 0 means no limit.

name: pica_gmail_update_a_user_s_gmail_language_settings
description: [GMAIL] Update a User’s Gmail Language Settings on gmail

displayLanguage.description: The language to display Gmail in, formatted as an RFC 3066 Language Tag (e.g., en-GB, fr, ja). Supported languages evolve; refer to the Gmail settings “Language” dropdown for available options.

_pica_metadata.action_title: Update a User’s Gmail Language Settings

name: pica_gmail_update_a_user_s_pop_settings
description: [GMAIL] Update a User’s POP Settings on gmail

accessWindow.description: The range of messages which are accessible via POP.

disposition.description: The action executed on a message after it has been fetched via POP.

name: pica_gmail_get_a_user_s_thread
description: [GMAIL] Get a User’s Thread on gmail

path.description: Path parameters for /gmail/v1/users/{userId}/threads/{id}

id.description: The ID of the thread to retrieve. (Send in URL path)

userId.description: The user's email address. The special value `me` can be used to indicate the authenticated user. Default value: `me`. (Send in URL path)

path.title: Path Parameters

query.description: Optional query parameters for the request URL.

format.description: The format to return the messages in. Default: `full`.

metadataHeaders.description: When given and `format` is METADATA, only include headers specified. Repeated query parameter. (Send in URL query string)

name: pica_gmail_list_a_user_s_gmail_threads
description: [GMAIL] List a User’s Gmail Threads on gmail

headers.description: HTTP headers to send with the request.
headers.title: Headers

path.description: Parameters to be substituted into the request path.

userId.description: The user's email address. The special value `me` can be used to indicate the authenticated user. Default value: `me`.

path.title: Path Parameters

query.description: URL query parameters for filtering and pagination.

includeSpamTrash.description: Include threads from `SPAM` and `TRASH` in the results. Default: `false`.

labelIds.description: Only return threads with labels that match all of the specified label IDs. (Repeated query parameter.)

maxResults.description: Maximum number of threads to return. Default: `100`. Maximum allowed: `500`.

pageToken.description: Page token to retrieve a specific page of results in the list.

q.description: Only return threads matching the specified query. Supports the same query format as the Gmail search box (e.g., "from:someuser@example.com rfc822msgid: is:unread"). Cannot be used when accessing the API using the `gmail.metadata` scope.

query.title: Query Parameters

removeLabelIds.description: A list of IDs of labels to remove from this thread. You can remove up to 100 labels with each update.

name: pica_gmail_set_up_a_user_mailbox_push_notification_watch
description: [GMAIL] Set Up a User Mailbox Push Notification Watch on gmail

labelFilterAction.description: **Deprecated.** Filtering behavior for the `labelIds` list. Values: `include`, `exclude`. Deprecated due to incorrect behavior in some cases; use `labelFilterBehavior` instead.

labelFilterBehavior.description: Filtering behavior for the `labelIds` list. Values: `include` (only changes relating to `labelIds`), `exclude` (all changes except those relating to `labelIds`). If set, `labelFilterAction` is ignored.

labelIds.description: List of label IDs to restrict notifications about. If omitted, all changes are pushed. If specified, dictates which labels are required for a push notification to be generated.

topicName.description: Fully qualified Pub/Sub topic name to publish events to. Must already exist and Gmail must have publish permission. Example: `projects/my-project-identifier/topics/my-topic-name`.

query.description: Parameters to be sent in the URL query string.

Enum meanings: `minimal` = only message ID and labels; no headers/body/payload. `full` = full message with body parsed in `payload`; `raw` not used. `raw` = full message in `message.raw` as base64url; `payload` not used. `metadata` = message ID, labels, and headers only.

name: pica_gmail_create_a_user_gmail_filter
description: [GMAIL] Create a User Gmail Filter on gmail

action.description: A set of actions to perform on a message.

addLabelIds.description: List of labels to add to the message.

forward.description: Email address that the message should be forwarded to.

removeLabelIds.description: List of labels to remove from the message.

action.title: Filter Actions

excludeChats.description: Whether the response should exclude chats.

from.description: The sender's display name or email address.

hasAttachment.description: Whether the message has any attachment.

negatedQuery.description: Only match messages not matching the specified query. Supports the same query format as the Gmail search box.

query.description: Only match messages matching the specified query. Supports the same query format as the Gmail search box.

size.description: The size of the entire RFC822 message in bytes, including all headers and attachments.

sizeComparison.description: How the message size in bytes should relate to criteria.size.

subject.description: Case-insensitive phrase found in the message's subject. Trailing/leading whitespace are trimmed; adjacent spaces are collapsed.

to.description: The recipient's display name or email address. Includes recipients in the "to", "cc", and "bcc" header fields.

id.description: The ID of the filter to be deleted.

userId.description: User's email address. The special value "me" can be used to indicate the authenticated user. Default: "me".

name: pica_gmail_update_a_user_s_auto_forwarding_settings
description: [GMAIL] Update a User’s Auto-Forwarding Settings on gmail

disposition.description: The state that a message should be left in after it has been forwarded.

emailAddress.description: Email address to which all incoming messages are forwarded. This email address must be a verified member of the forwarding addresses.

enabled.description: Whether all incoming mail is automatically forwarded to another address.

name: pica_gmail_update_user_vacation_responder_settings
description: [GMAIL] Update User Vacation Responder Settings on gmail

enableAutoReply.description: Flag that controls whether Gmail automatically replies to messages.

endTime.description: Optional end time for sending auto-replies (epoch ms). If both startTime and endTime are specified, startTime must precede endTime.

responseBodyHtml.description: Response body in HTML format. Gmail will sanitize the HTML before storing it. If both responseBodyPlainText and responseBodyHtml are specified, responseBodyHtml will be used.

responseBodyPlainText.description: Response body in plain text format. If both responseBodyPlainText and responseBodyHtml are specified, responseBodyHtml will be used.

responseSubject.description: Optional text to prepend to the subject line in vacation responses. To enable auto-replies, either responseSubject or a response body must be nonempty.

restrictToContacts.description: Flag that determines whether responses are sent to recipients who are not in the user's list of contacts.

restrictToDomain.description: Flag that determines whether responses are sent to recipients who are outside of the user's domain. This feature is only available for Google Workspace users.

startTime.description: Optional start time for sending auto-replies (epoch ms). If both startTime and endTime are specified, startTime must precede endTime.

name: pica_gmail_stop_push_notifications_for_a_gmail_user
description: [GMAIL] Stop Push Notifications for a Gmail User on gmail

name: pica_gmail_permanently_delete_a_user_s_thread
description: [GMAIL] Permanently Delete a User’s Thread on gmail

path.description: Path parameters to construct /gmail/v1/users/{userId}/threads/{id}. Both must be present and non-empty; userId defaults to "me" if not provided by the user.

id.description: ID of the thread to delete.

userId.description: The user's email address. The special value `me` can be used to indicate the authenticated user. Default value: `me`.

path.title: Path Parameters
